Off Calendar Dec-08-2009 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Sep-14-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CLARINDO GOMES, and, BLACK HAWK CAR SERVICE LIMOS & BUSSES, LLC Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Appearances: Lawrence William Fasano, Esq. Law Offices of Lawrence William Fasano, (415) 956-8800, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Servando Sandoval, Esq. of Pahl & McCay (408) 286-5100 appearing for Defendants and cross-complainants, and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for preliminary injunction denied. Defendants fail to show a reasonable probability of prevailing on the merits or that the balance of harm weighs in favor of issuing the injunction. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Mary Ellen Scherer, CSR #5208

Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Sep-08-2009 Continued To Sep-14-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CLARINDO GOMES, and, BLACK HAWK CAR SERVICE LIMOS & BUSSES, LLC Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Injunction. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Continued to September 14, 2009. Moving party to provide courtesy copy of moving papers and reply brief with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Plaintiffs Alexandre Pupo Gouvea And Paulo Henrique Rosetto'S Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Apr-07-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiffs Alexandre Pupo Gouvea and Paulo Henrique Rosetto's Order to Show Cause regarding Preliminary Injunction (per ex parte Order of 3/23/09). Appearances: Lawrence W. Fasano, Jr., Esq. of the Fasano Law Office (415) 956-8800, appearing for Plaintiffs and moving party; Servando Sandoval, Esq., of Pahl & McCay, (408) 286-5100, appearing for Defendants Clarindo Gomes and Black Hawk Car Service Limos and Busses, LLC, and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for preliminary injunction is denied. Plaintiffs have not shown that they are likely to prevail on the merits; no showing of imminent, irreparable harm. Defense Counsel will prepare Order. (302CWW/VC)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
